title: Write a Ministerial Brief
description: Draft a short ministerial brief that frames a political issue, lays out options, and recommends one path with risks.
whyItMatters: Politics and policy careers are writing careers. A good brief turns complexity into judgment-ready advice.

steps.title: Define the issue
steps.description: State the problem clearly in plain English and note why it is urgent now.

steps.title: Lay out options
steps.description: Offer at least three options, including risks, cost considerations, and political tradeoffs.

steps.title: Recommend a path
steps.description: Make one recommendation and justify it in a way a minister could act on.

steps.title: Add implementation notes
steps.description: State what would need to happen next: consultation, Cabinet, legislation, funding, or regulation.

deliverables: A one-page implementation and risk appendix

rubric.description: Could a decision-maker understand the issue quickly and trust the structure?
Does the recommendation show tradeoff awareness rather than pure advocacy?
